title: 重命名扩展元数据与配置命名空间
summary: Renamed the extension contribution-point surface from ralph-runner to harness-runner across package metadata, command ids, chat participant ids/names, configuration keys, runtime registration, localized menu links, tests, and README guidance while preserving .harness-runner as the runtime artifact root.
architectureNotes: Package metadata, activation events, command ids, chat participant ids, and configuration keys are a single VS Code contribution-point cluster and were renamed together to avoid manifest/runtime drift.
The runtime artifact root stays .harness-runner; this story renames symbolic contribution points around that stable persistence boundary instead of moving artifact storage paths.

src/extension.ts remains the orchestration entrypoint, while the rename-sensitive prompt copy in src/projectConstraints.ts stays within its existing responsibility so /harness-spec wording remains cohesive without spreading chat-specific strings across new modules.
Localization, README, and tests were updated in the same pass because command-id and participant-name changes are only reviewable when UI copy and assertions move with the runtime values.

The rollback seam is a single story-scoped commit touching manifest/runtime/test/docs surfaces together; reverting only package.json without extension.ts and localization.ts would leave broken command routing.
keyDecisions: Adopt harness-runner as the only active extension/package/configuration namespace in package.json and runtime registration code.
Rename the chat participant to recent-graduates.harness-runner with the @harness /harness-spec command so the manifest and runtime handler stay aligned.
Keep workspace settings migration narrow by deleting old ralph-runner workspace keys when persisting .vscode/settings.json overrides and writing only harness-runner keys going forward.
Keep the existing interrupted src/projectConstraints.ts rename work and align tests to it instead of reverting partially completed Harness Runner prompt copy.
constraintsConfirmed: No dist/ or out/ files were hand-edited.
At least one project constraint test command succeeded before completion.
Git commit title and description must use Chinese.
risks: package.json repository.url and the checked-in VSIX filename still reference the legacy ralph-runner slug, so packaging/distribution branding is not fully normalized yet.
Broader RALPH-branded runtime copy still exists outside the contribution-point namespace surface and will need a later rebrand story to remove mixed terminology end to end.
The generated/manual project-constraints artifacts still mention the old package name and should be refreshed in a later maintenance pass if those artifacts are used as operator truth.
followUps: Regenerate the packaged VSIX and decide whether the repository slug should be renamed or intentionally left as-is.
Refresh .harness-runner project-constraints and Agent Map artifacts so operator guidance reflects the new harness-runner package namespace.
Continue the broader user-visible brand sweep in later stories for remaining RALPH copy that is outside this contribution-point rename scope.
